topic: How to build a sync-ready catalogue from scratch
hook: Building a sync-ready catalogue isn't a weekend project.
cta: Start this process now. Don't wait for a brief.
caption_hook: Building a sync-ready catalogue isn't a weekend project. ↓
caption_full:
Building a sync-ready catalogue isn't a weekend project. ↓

But it's not complicated.

Start this process now. Don't wait for a brief.

#SyncMaster #AfricanComposers #MusicBusiness #SyncReady

slides.headline: Building a sync-ready catalogue isn't a weekend project.

slides.body:
But it's not complicated. It's sequential. This process takes 3–5 weeks done properly.
Every brief you respond to after that takes a fraction of the time.
Here's the exact 5-step process we use at SyncMaster — from application to brief-ready. ↓

STEP 1: RIGHTS AUDIT (Weeks 1–2)
Before anything else: confirm what you own.

STEP 2: FILE PREPARATION (Weeks 2–3)
→ Export full lossless WAV for every sync-ready track
→ File naming convention: Artist_Title_BPM_Key.wav
→ Export stems for

STEP 3: METADATA TAGGING (Week 3)
→ ISRC assigned to every recording
→ BPM, key, duration, genre embedded in file metadata
→ Mood tags: 3–5 per track in industry

slides.headline: Open briefs. Every Tuesday.
slides.body: Composers: apply once, get matched for life. Supervisors: shortlist in 48h.
